Monticalia nigrostagnosa is a plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) native to the Andean region of South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, potentially providing health benefits in the management of conditions such as arthritis and cardiovascular disease. Bioactive constituents of Monticalia nigrostagnosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Monticalia nigrostagnosa are commonly used in traditional medicine in the Andean region, and may include teas, infusions, and extracts.
